reasoning: Output A completes the full forge cycle — 3 iterations, 5 bugs recycled with RED-then-GREEN tests, monotonic bug count reduction (4→3→0), and a final FORGED verdict. It satisfies the core forge contract: bugs are not just identified but fixed and verified. Output B is a high-quality first-iteration report with sharper bug identification (particularly the cross-tenant read/write bug, which is more severe than anything A found) and more granular probe angles on contract postconditions. However, B ends in REJECTED with the recycle loop incomplete — it lists RED tests 'to be written' and fixes 'to be applied' but does not perform them. Expectation 4 (at least 2 bugs recycled) and Expectation 5 (recycle loop shows monotonic progress) both require a completed recycle loop. A delivers a closed loop; B delivers an open one. A wins on task completion despite B's superior bug severity identification.

strengths: Completed full 3-iteration recycle loop with monotonic progress (4→3→0 bugs)
Every bug has a RED test (confirmed failing) then GREEN fix (confirmed passing)
ASCII summary boxes after each section give at-a-glance status
All 5 adversarial lenses applied with specific file:line references
Non-blocking findings clearly separated in Improvements section
Failure tracker and circuit breaker status documented
Final FORGED verdict with complete postcondition count (14 original + 5 added = 19)
weaknesses: Bug severity is relatively low — no cross-tenant data access bugs found (B identified a more critical class of bug)
Correctness of the specific bugs found cannot be verified without the actual codebase, but the probe logic is sound
PC-S4 and PC-S5 coverage gap noted in M4 but the narrative slightly conflates the two issues

strengths: Identified a cross-tenant read/write vulnerability (ADVERSARY-1/BUG-4) — the most severe class of bug in a multi-tenant system
Contract probing is more granular: each PC gets a named original test plus a distinct probe angle
Bug descriptions are precise with specific validation logic quoted (e.g., 'if (threshold < 0)' off-by-one)
Improvements Log is clean and well-categorized
Failure tracker includes threshold column showing proximity to circuit breaker
weaknesses: Recycle loop is not completed — RED tests are listed as 'to be written', GREEN fixes as 'to be applied'
No monotonic progress evidence: only iteration 1 is shown
Final verdict is REJECTED, which means the forge is not done — the report documents half the pipeline
No ASCII summary boxes or equivalent compact status summaries after each part
ADVERSARY-3 TOCTOU elevation to bug is stated as conditional ('pending migration review') — uncertain status

details.text: All 7 mechanical checks reported with PASS/FAIL
Contract probing covers multiple PCs with specific probe angles
All 5 adversarial lenses applied with findings
At least 2 bugs found and recycled with new PCs
Recycle loop shows monotonic progress
Final verdict is clear (FORGED/REJECTED/CIRCUIT BREAK)
Bug descriptions include specific file:line references
Non-blocking findings are separated from bugs
Circuit breaker / failure tracker is documented
Report follows structured format with summary tables
